title: Construction and Culture
catalogDescription: Evolution of building construction through history, cultures, economies, and institutions. Explores roles of people in building construction, with emphasis on the roles of owner, builder, and designer through history up to the present time. Focuses on worldwide projects and the people who built them in the context of cultural, economic, scientific, technological, and professional developments of the era. Offered: AWSp.

title: Digital Tools
catalogDescription: Methods of depicting geometric, cost and time information about built environment projects to communicate information necessary to project management. Development of three-dimensional and data analysis skills through digital tools used to support major design and construction processes. Prerequisite: either MATH 111, MATH 112, MATH 120, or MATH 124. Offered: AWSp.

title: Construction Methods and Materials III
catalogDescription: Analysis of construction methods and materials. Emphasizes techniques for sitework, foundations, and earthmoving, including classification and physical properties of soil and study of the basic principles, practices, and techniques used in the construction industry for selecting and managing construction equipment. Includes a weekly laboratory with emphasis on soils, foundations, site work, site surveying, and earthmoving. Course overlaps with: CM 332 and CM 432. Prerequisite: CM 323. Offered: Sp.
